name: West Indian Rice and Peas with Tempeh
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dried black eyed peas (soaked for at least 5 hours)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, sauté rice and coconut for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
2. Add water and cinnamon stick. Cover the pot and bring to a rapid boil.
3. Do not peek at the rice; when steam starts escaping, turn the heat down.
4. Simmer for 40 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, cook black eyed peas with bay leaves in salted, boiling water until tender (about 20-25 minutes). Drain and keep warm.
6. In a separate pan, sauté garlic and onion in oil until onions soften.
7. Stir in chile and bell pepper. Cook for 2 minutes.
8. Add fennel, tempeh, salt, and pepper. Lower heat, stirring frequently until tempeh is crisp and golden.
9. Combine everything, mixing well.
